---
title: "Dynamical Systems"
subtitle: "A Spring-Mass Example"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    slide-number: false
    chalkboard: true
    incremental: false
    toc: false
    logo: ../../img/Charite.png
    footer: "TVB Workshop · Mathematical Framework"
    link-external-newwindow: true
    highlight-style: github
    include-in-header: ../../_slides-home-btn.html
    fig-align: center
execute:
  enabled: false
---

## The Spring Equation {background-color="#ffffff"}

$$\dot{x} = v \qquad \dot{v} = -\frac{k}{m}\,x$$

![](phase_space.png){fig-align="center" height="440px"}

## A Dynamical System {background-color="#ffffff"}

![](spring_single.gif){.fragment fig-align="center" height="580px"}

## Initial Conditions {background-color="#ffffff"}

![](spring_ics.gif){.fragment fig-align="center" height="580px"}

## Phase Space {background-color="#ffffff"}

![](phase_space.png){fig-align="center" height="580px"}

## Parameters — Mass {background-color="#ffffff"}

![](spring_mass.gif){.fragment fig-align="center" height="580px"}

## Parameters — Stiffness {background-color="#ffffff"}

![](spring_stiffness.gif){.fragment fig-align="center" height="580px"}
